findings.message: Line 24 names the ADR front-matter convention as `knowledge/adr/adr.md`, but the same ADR later identifies the same convention/update target as `knowledge/conventions/adr.md` in Consequences/Related. This is an internal cross-document reference contradiction that makes the implementation target ambiguous; align the Context reference with the convention path used elsewhere.

D4 makes the per-decision grounding adjudication a work item of the track implementing this ADR, but the briefing defines that adjudication of the six flipped decisions as a manual follow-up that is out of scope for this track's code. As written, downstream planning can treat those unrelated ADR front-matter edits as required implementation work for this track, contradicting the intended scope. Clarify D4 so the current track only changes the precedence/value-object behavior and leaves the six-decision adjudication to the separate manual follow-up.
